justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Three distinct integration patterns clearly identified and designed — SAP as batch/SFTP/XML with polling consumer, DHL as webhook/REST with async gateway, Analytics as Kafka streaming. (2) DHL 500ms constraint explicitly addressed via dedicated Webhook Gateway that persists to Kafka and returns 200 immediately. (3) DHL webhook idempotency addressed multiple times — in risks, pitfalls_to_avoid, and patterns_to_follow. (4) SAP silent failure risk explicitly identified with staleness check alerting and file-level checksums. (5) Dedicated integration layer recommended — each adapter is an independent service with ACL, no integration logic in domain services. (6) Flexibility points explicitly call out adding FedEx as a new adapter with no changes to existing components.

observations.text: Excellent separation of the Webhook Gateway as a distinct component from the DHL Adapter — this is a sophisticated design choice that cleanly solves the 500ms SLA
Comprehensive risk analysis covering all expected failure modes plus additional ones (schema evolution, dual-write, consumer lag)
Transactional Outbox pattern inclusion shows depth — addresses the dual-write problem that many architects miss
Implementation guidance is practical and sequenced correctly — starting with the tightest constraint (DHL 500ms) first
DDD context map with proper relationship types (Customer-Supplier, Conformist, ACL) demonstrates strong domain modeling
Output is extremely verbose — while thorough, the level of detail goes well beyond what was needed to demonstrate competence on the scenario

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an Anti-Corruption Layer per external system, an internal event backbone on Kafka, and dedicated adapter components that translate between external protocols/formats and the platform's canonical domain events. The design prioritizes loose coupling, independent evolvability of each integration, and operational re
